- [2026-07-01T08:54:03Z] Taylor/finding — buy-order cancel-replace churn (identical px/qty): {"job": "Taylor_20260701_085032", "question": "SpaceX 2026-07-01 go-live: >100 buy orders placed/cancelled/re-placed with unchanged qty+price — deliberate strategy? effective?", "verdict": "NOT a strategy. Unintended side-effect of the periodic slice-refresh loop with NO change-detection guard. Net-negative (queue-priority churn), zero fills on affected names.", "evidence": {"journal": "data/execution_logs/exec_SpaceX_2026-07-01_journal.csv (3483 rows)", "action_counts": {"PLACE": 310, "CANCEL_STALE": 240, "PLACE_FAIL": 2785, "WAIT_QUOTA": 101, "CANCEL_FAIL": 23, "FILL": 12, "DONE": 12}, "churn_pattern": "9 BUY tickers (VPB VIB VGC TPB MBB HDB DCM CTG BID) each PLACEd 33x with distinct_px=1, distinct_qty=1 across ALL cycles = byte-identical order re-placed ~32x every 8min from 10:00 to 14:50. 9x32=~240 = exactly the CANCEL_STALE count.", "example_VPB": "qty=2800 px=27150 every cycle, filled=0 all day. 27150 = ref_price(26750)x1.015 rounded = the max_chase_pct_buy cap.", "fill_contrast": "The 9 churners: 0 fills. The 12 that DID fill (ACB HPG VCB VHM VIX VND...) each placed ONCE and DONE. Churning never produced a single fill."}, "root_cause": {"mechanism": "_cancel_stale (executor.py:328) cancels any open child older than slice_interval_min(8m) UNCONDITIONALLY by age. _place_slices (executor.py:505) then recomputes px via _limit_price and qty via _child_qty from the live quote and re-places. NO check that the recomputed order differs from the resting one.", "why_identical": "px=min(desired_ask, cap) where cap=ref_price*(1+max_chase_pct_buy=1.5%) is a CONSTANT. On go-live the market gapped >1.5% above stale plan ref, so live ask>cap all session -> px pins to the constant cap every cycle. qty also constant (max_child_value/px and 10%-participation both non-binding -> child=full parent qty). Recompute -> identical -> cancel+replace is a no-op.", "harm": "HOSE continuous session = price-time (FIFO) priority. Cancel+new re-place sends the order to the BACK of the queue at that price level every 8min -> strictly reduces fill probability for competitively-priced resting orders. Today the 9 were capped BELOW market (never fillable regardless), so the churn cost no fills TODAY but is pure API waste; on an at-market resting order it WOULD forfeit real fill priority."}, "separate_issues_found": {"PLACE_FAIL_2785": "NOT the churn. 1494 = HTTP400 Invalid price lot (concentrated SHS+MBS 807 each — HNX/UPCOM tick-size mismatch, retried every poll); 1291 = trading-token expired (all order ops rejected until OTP re-auth). Both are separate bugs worth a look.", "chase_cap_too_tight": "The +1.5% buy cap left the entire core bank book (9 names) un-fillable on a gap-up day -> 0 fills on those. Plan-ref vs market gap issue for DollarBill/Mike, distinct from the churn."}, "proposed_fix_DO_NOT_SELF_APPLY": {"minimal": "In the stale-refresh path, before cancelling, compute the would-be (px,qty) that _place_slices would produce; if identical to the resting child (px equal, qty within 1 lot), DO NOT cancel — just reset the child ts (age clock) to keep FIFO priority. Only cancel+replace when new px differs by >=1 tick or qty by >=1 lot. Preserves chase-on-move, kills identity churn.", "caveat": "Production LIVE (SpaceX). Change to _cancel_stale must go through quant-skeptic + threads=1 replay before wiring. Proposal only; no code changed."}}

- [2026-07-01T10:22:26Z] Taylor/finding — patch#3 static chase-cap → volatility-scaled (recommend): {"job": "Taylor_20260701_102033", "question": "max_chase_pct_buy=1.5% static cap → whole 9-bank basket 0-fill on gap-up go-live day. Keep, widen, or vol-scale?", "verdict": "NOT keep as-is. Intent (anti-overpay) is right; a FLAT % across a cross-section of different vols is the wrong instrument.", "core_critique": "1.5% is volatility-blind. For a 1%/day low-vol name it is ~1.5 sigma (real discipline); for a bank at ~2.2-2.5%/day it is ~0.6 sigma (noise) → banks are STRUCTURALLY unfillable on ANY firm open, not just extreme gaps. That is why the entire highest-beta sub-universe went 0/9 while lower-vol names filled — systematic, not random.", "alpha_leak": "BAL book is momentum (SIGNAL_V11). Momentum fires on names that just broke out = names most likely to gap/run = exactly the names the flat cap rejects. Execution-selection bias fighting the strategy own alpha → silent low-beta tilt in the realized book that NO backtest models (backtests assume fill at plan price). This is the real reason to fix, beyond the one-day miss.", "Q1_keep": "No. Failure mode is binary TOTAL miss on the highest-conviction slice all session, not paying-slightly-less. Mis-specified control, not acceptable cost of discipline.", "Q2_vol_vs_widen": "Vol-scale, decisively. Bumping to 2-2.5% is a band-aid — relocates the same cross-sectional error (too loose for low-vol, still too tight for hot banks). rvol_20d already loaded per-ticker in _gap_ref at startup — infra exists.", "Q3_allocator_link": "Execution-layer only; independent of rating_8l/custom30V (selection) and allocator/w_LAG (sizing). BUT subtle: leaving it corrupts the map backtest→live book (unintended low-beta drift). Structurally independent, economically coupled.", "recommendation": "Volatility-scaled cap: cap_pct = clamp(k * rvol_20d, floor=0.015, ceil=0.04); cap = ref_price*(1+cap_pct). k~2.0 (~2 sigma of a 1-day move). FAIL-SAFE: rvol_20d missing/<=0 → fall back to static 1.5% (reuse existing _gap_ref fail-safe pattern, zero change when data absent).", "why": "(a) reuses tested infra (rvol_20d), no new data dep. (b) MONOTONE-SAFE: floor=1.5% means it can only WIDEN vs today, never tighten → cannot degrade any name that currently fills; clean A/B (fill-rate strictly >=, price never worse on already-filling names). (c) numeric: bank 2.2%/day→cap 4% (ceil); utility 1%/day→2%; defensive 0.7%/day→floored 1.5%. Spread is exactly right.", "self_pushback": "Widening raises realized entry cost — discipline exists for a reason. MUST backtest NET effect (fill-rate-weighted entry-quality over gap-up days), not just fill-rate. For momentum, missing a breakout >> 3% of entry, so likely net-positive, but MEASURE. Gate behind flag, default = current 1.5%, PAPER-first, user sign-off before LIVE (same convention as gap_adaptive/extreme_regime).", "connects_to": "This is the ROOT CAUSE of Taylor 08:54 finding (>100 cancel/replace identical px/qty = the 0-fill banks). Churn-guard (patch#1) stops the wasted churn; vol-scale cap fixes WHY they never fill. Two halves of the same pathology — ship together.", "out_of_scope_flag": "Symmetric risk on sell side (max_chase_pct_sell=3%, gap-down stranding) — partly covered by EXTREME sell-to-floor; not expanded here."}
